root_text.ud1.3:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā uruvelāyaṁ viharati najjā nerañjarāya tīre bodhirukkhamūle paṭhamābhisambuddho.
root_text.ud1.3:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā sattāhaṁ ekapallaṅkena nisinno hoti vimuttisukhapaṭisaṁvedī.
root_text.ud1.3:1.4: Atha kho bhagavā tassa sattāhassa accayena tamhā samādhimhā vuṭṭhahitvā rattiyā pacchimaṁ yāmaṁ paṭiccasamuppādaṁ anulomapaṭilomaṁ sādhukaṁ manasākāsi:
root_text.ud1.3:2.1: “Iti imasmiṁ sati idaṁ hoti, imassuppādā idaṁ uppajjati,
root_text.ud1.3:2.2: imasmiṁ asati idaṁ na hoti, imassa nirodhā idaṁ nirujjhati; yadidaṁ—
root_text.ud1.3:4.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud1.3:1.2: At one time, when he was first awakened, the Buddha was staying in Uruvelā at the root of the tree of awakening on the bank of the Nerañjarā River.
translation_text.ud1.3:1.3: There the Buddha sat cross-legged for seven days without moving, experiencing the bliss of freedom.
translation_text.ud1.3:1.4: When seven days had passed, the Buddha emerged from that state of immersion. In the last watch, he carefully applied the mind to dependent origination in forward and reverse order:
translation_text.ud1.3:2.1: “When this exists, this comes to be; due to the arising of this, this arises.
translation_text.ud1.3:2.2: When this doesn’t exist, this doesn’t come to be; due to the cessation of this, this ceases. That is:
translation_text.ud1.3:2.3: Ignorance is a requirement for choices.
translation_text.ud1.3:2.4: Choices are a requirement for consciousness.
translation_text.ud1.3:2.5: Consciousness is a requirement for name and form.
translation_text.ud1.3:2.6: Name and form are requirements for the six sense fields.
translation_text.ud1.3:2.7: The six sense fields are requirements for contact.
translation_text.ud1.3:2.8: Contact is a requirement for feeling.
translation_text.ud1.3:2.9: Feeling is a requirement for craving.
translation_text.ud1.3:2.10: Craving is a requirement for grasping.
translation_text.ud1.3:2.11: Grasping is a requirement for continued existence.
translation_text.ud1.3:2.12: Continued existence is a requirement for rebirth.
translation_text.ud1.3:2.13: Rebirth is a requirement for old age and death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress to come to be.
translation_text.ud1.3:2.14: That is how this entire mass of suffering originates.
translation_text.ud1.3:3.1: When ignorance fades away and ceases with nothing left over, choices cease.
translation_text.ud1.3:3.3: When consciousness ceases, name and form cease.
translation_text.ud1.3:3.4: When name and form cease, the six sense fields cease.
translation_text.ud1.3:3.5: When the six sense fields cease, contact ceases.
translation_text.ud1.3:3.9: When grasping ceases, continued existence ceases.
translation_text.ud1.3:3.10: When continued existence ceases, rebirth ceases.
translation_text.ud1.3:3.11: When rebirth ceases, old age and death, sorrow, lamentation, pain, sadness, and distress cease.
translation_text.ud1.3:3.12: That is how this entire mass of suffering ceases.”
translation_text.ud1.3:4.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud1.3:5.4: as the sun lights up the sky.”

reference_text.ud1.3:1.1: cck25.65, csp1ed18.79, csp2ed18.79, ms18Ud_12, msdiv3, vri47.71
